figures.caption: Table 1: SLT results on PHOENIX14T and CSL-Daily datasets. The best results are highlighted as bold, and the second-best are underlined.

Table 2: How2Sign and OpenASL datasets. T5-SLT-YT denotes training on YouTube-ASL and then fine-tuning on the 30-hour split How2Sign. The best results are highlighted as bold, and the second-best are underlined.

Figure 2: An overview of the SignThought framework, which consists of three parts: (i) a sign encoder that maps the input video X into dense evidence features E; (ii) a Latent Chain-of-Thought module that iteratively updates K learnable thought slots and performs structured routing (segmentation→ Sinkhorn-style binding→ routed retrieval, with an optional monotonic bias) to distill E into an ordered thought chain C; and (iii) a plan-then-ground dualstream decoder that first attends to C for semantic planning and then grounds each token on E under thought-guided temporal priors to generate the spoken-language translation end-to-end.
Table 6: Translation results. Correct answers are marked in green, semantically similar but rephrased answers are displayed in blue, and incorrect answers are indicated in red.
